name: Parmesan-Style Eggplant
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Cooked pinto beans or chickpeas
2 ounces to 4 ounces soft goat's milk cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and slice the eggplant lengthwise into 1/2-inch (1/4") slabs. Brush both sides of all pieces with olive oil. Arrange on a cookie sheet in a 9x13-inch pan.
2. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes. Set aside.
3. Place pinto beans or chick-peas (I used the chick peas, next time I'd mash all of them as the whole pea texture was not exactly what I had in mind) into a bowl or dish. Mash about half of them with a fork. Set aside.
4. In a large frying pan, saute the onions in 1 tablespoon olive oil until soft. Add the mushrooms (I used a 4.5-ounce can, drained) and garlic. Cook until the liquid that the mushrooms have released has evaporated. Set aside.
5. Crumble the cheese, set aside. I used shredded mild cheddar and shredded Mozzarella/Provolone mix - it was what I had in the fridge.
6. Slice the zucchini lengthwise into 1/4-inch (1 cm) slabs. Steam for 7 minutes, then drain on a cotton towel. I nuked mine for about 4 minutes total. Set aside.
7. To assemble, oil a 9x13-inch baking dish. Arrange the eggplant slices in one layer in the bottom. Cover with the beans. Sprinkle beans with half the oregano (another substitution - baby started crying so since Italian seasoning was the only one close at hand used that instead of oregano - next time I'll try it with just oregano as suggested).
8. Spread on the mushroom mixture, and top it with a layer of cheese.
9. Sprinkle cheese with remaining oregano. Layer on the zucchini, then cover with tomato sauce (here I used Ragu garden with mushrooms) and crumbs.
10. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes. Allow to stand for 10 minutes before cutting into squares and serving.
Note: You can assemble the casserole and refrigerate until ready to bake. Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes.
If you omit the cheese, serve with a nut or seed dessert for added protein.
